self.current_transition_state.is_exiting_scope()\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Check if the parser is at the same scope level\n149\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n150\t        self.current_transition_state.is_same_level()\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n156\t    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n157\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications and transition tracking.\n158\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n159\t        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n160\t\n161\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n162\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n163\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n164\t\n165\t            // Skip document markers and directives\n166\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n167\t                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker line)\n168\t                self.current_line_type = LineClassification::Empty;\n169\t                continue;\n170\t            }\n171\t\n172\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n173\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n174\t\n175\t            // Track line type in parser state\n176\t            self.current_line_type = line_type;\n177\t\n178\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n179\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n180\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n181\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n182\t\n183\t                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n184\t                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n185\t                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n186\t            }\n187\t        }\n188\t\n189\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n190\t    }\n191\t\n192\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n193\t    ///\n194\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n195\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n196\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n197\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n198\t\n199\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n200\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n201\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n202\t\n203\t        (enter, exit, same)\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n209\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n210\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n211\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n212\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n213\t\n214\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n215\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n216\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n217\t\n218\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n219\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n220\t\n221\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n222\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n223\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n224\t                {\n225\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n226\t                }\n227\t                scope_stack.reset();\n228\t                continue;\n229\t            }\n230\t\n231\t            // Skip YAML directives\n232\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n233\t                continue;\n234\t            }\n235\t\n236\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n237\t\n238\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n239\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n240\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n241\t            if indent_changed {\n242\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n243\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n244\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n245\t            }\n246\t\n247\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n248\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n249\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n250\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n251\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n252\t                // Process indent transitions for empty/indent-only lines\n253\t                // Comments are treated as indent-only for scope tracking purposes\n254\t                if indent_changed && line_type.is_empty() {\n255\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n256\t                }\n257\t                continue;\n258\t            }\n259\t\n260\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n261\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n262\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n263\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n264\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n265\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n266\t                }\n267\t                continue;\n268\t            }\n269\t\n270\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n271\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n272\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n273\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n274\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n275\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n276\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n277\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n278\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n279\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n280\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n281\t                                    dup_err.message()\n282\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n283\t                            }\n284\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n285\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n286\t                            {\n287\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n288\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n289\t                            }\n290\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n291\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n292\t                                line_num_1index,\n293\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n294\t                            );\n295\t                        } else {\n296\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n297\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n298\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n299\t                            {\n300\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n301\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n302\t                            }\n303\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n304\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n305\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n306\t                                    dup_err.message()\n307\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n308\t                            }\n309\t                        }\n310\t                    } else {\n311\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n312\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n313\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n314\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n315\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n316\t                        {\n317\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n318\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n319\t                        }\n320\t                    }\n321\t                }\n322\t                Ordering::Less => {\n323\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n324\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n325\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n326\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n327\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n328\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n329\t\n330\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n331\t                    {\n332\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n333\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n334\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n335\t                    }\n336\t\n337\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n338\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n339\t                    }\n340\t\n341\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n342\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n343\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n344\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n345\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n346\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n347\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n348\t                                    dup_err.message()\n349\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n350\t                            }\n351\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n352\t                            {\n353\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n354\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n355\t                            }\n356\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n357\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n358\t                                line_num_1index,\n359\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n360\t                            );\n361\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n362\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n363\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n364\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n365\t                                    dup_err.message()\n366\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n367\t                            }\n368\t                        }\n369\t                    }\n370\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n371\t                }\n372\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n373\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n374\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n375\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n376\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n377\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n378\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n379\t                            {\n380\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n381\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n382\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n383\t                            }\n384\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n385\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n386\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n387\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n388\t                                    dup_err.message()\n389\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n390\t                            }\n391\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n392\t                            {\n393\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n394\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n395\t                            }\n396\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n397\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n398\t                                line_num_1index,\n399\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n400\t                            );\n401\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n402\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n403\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n404\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n405\t                                    dup_err.message()\n406\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n407\t                            }\n408\t                        }\n409\t                    }\n410\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n411\t                }\n412\t            }\n413\t\n414\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n415\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n416\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n417\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n418\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n419\t                    {\n420\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n421\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n422\t                    }\n423\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n424\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n425\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n426\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n427\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n428\t                            dup_err.message()\n429\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n430\t                    }\n431\t                } else {\n432\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n433\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n434\t                    {\n435\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n436\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n437\t                    }\n438\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n439\t                }\n440\t            }\n441\t        }\n442\t\n443\t        duplicate_errors\n444\t    }\n445\t}\n446\t\n447\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n448\t    fn default() -> Self {\n449\t        Self::new()\n450\t    }\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n454\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n455\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n456\t        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n457\t        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n458\t\n459\t        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n460\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n461\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n462\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n463\t\n464\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n465\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n466\t\n467\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n468\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n469\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n470\t                {\n471\t                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n472\t                }\n473\t                scope_stack.reset();\n474\t                continue;\n475\t            }\n476\t\n477\t            // Skip YAML directives\n478\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n479\t                continue;\n480\t            }\n481\t\n482\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n483\t\n484\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n485\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n486\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n487\t            if indent_changed {\n488\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n489\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n490\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n491\t            }\n492\t\n493\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n494\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n495\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n496\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n497\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n498\t                // Process indent transitions for empty/indent-only lines\n499\t                // Comments are treated as indent-only for scope tracking purposes\n500\t                if indent_changed && line_type.is_empty() {\n501\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n502\t                }\n503\t                continue;\n504\t            }\n505\t\n506\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n507\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n508\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n509\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n510\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n511\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n512\t                }\n513\t                continue;\n514\t            }\n515\t\n516\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n517\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n518\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n519\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n520\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n521\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n522\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n523\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n524\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n525\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n526\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n527\t                                    dup_err.message()\n528\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n529\t                            }\n530\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n531\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n532\t                            {\n533\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n534\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n535\t                            }\n536\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n537\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n538\t                                line_num_1index,\n539\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n540\t                            );\n541\t                        } else {\n542\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n543\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n544\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n545\t                            {\n546\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n547\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n548\t                            }\n549\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n550\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n551\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n552\t                                    dup_err.message()\n553\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n554\t                            }\n555\t                        }\n556\t                    } else {\n557\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n558\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n559\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n560\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n561\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n562\t                        {\n563\t                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n564\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n565\t                        }\n566\t                    }\n567\t                }\n568\t                Ordering::Less => {\n569\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n570\t                    // Record the indent transition with key\n571\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n572\t\n573\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n574\t                    {\n575\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n576\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n577\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n578\t                    }\n579\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n580\t\n581\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n582\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n583\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n584\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n585\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n586\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n587\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n588\t                                    dup_err.message()\n589\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n590\t                            }\n591\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n592\t                            {\n593\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n594\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n595\t                            }\n596\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n597\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n598\t                                line_num_1index,\n599\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n600\t                            );\n601\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n602\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n603\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n604\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n605\t                                    dup_err.message()\n606\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n607\t                            }\n608\t                        }\n609\t                    }\n610\t                }\n611\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n612\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n613\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n614\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n615\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n616\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n617\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n618\t                            {\n619\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n620\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n621\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n622\t                            }\n623\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n624\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n625\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n626\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n627\t                                    dup_err.message()\n628\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n629\t                            }\n630\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n631\t                            {\n632\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n633\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n634\t                            }\n635\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n636\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n637\t                                line_num_1index,\n638\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n639\t                            );\n640\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n641\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n642\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n643\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n644\t                                    dup_err.message()\n645\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n646\t                            }\n647\t                        }\n648\t                    }\n649\t                }\n650\t            }\n651\t\n652\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n653\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n654\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n655\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n656\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n657\t                    {\n658\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n659\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n660\t                    }\n661\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n662\t                }\n663\t\n664\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n665\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n666\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n667\t                    {\n668\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n669\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n670\t                    }\n671\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n672\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n673\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n674\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n675\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n676\t                            dup_err.message()\n677\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n678\t                    }\n679\t                } else {\n680\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n681\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n682\t                    {\n683\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n684\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n685\t                    }\n686\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n687\t                }\n688\t            }\n689\t        }\n690\t\n691\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n692\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n693\t            Ok(value) => {\n694\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n695\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n696\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n697\t            }\n698\t            Err(err) => {\n699\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n700\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n701\t                    err.to_string()\n702\t                } else {\n703\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n704\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n705\t                };\n706\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n707\t            }\n708\t        }\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n712\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n713\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n714\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n715\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n716\t        }\n717\t    }\n718\t\n719\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n720\t        // Read file content\n721\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n722\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n723\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n724\t        }\n725\t    }\n726\t\n727\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n728\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n729\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n730\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n731\t        } else {\n732\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n733\t        };\n734\t\n735\t        // Run syntax validation\n736\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n737\t\n738\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n739\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n740\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n741\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n742\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n743\t                result.valid = false;\n744\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n745\t            }\n746\t        }\n747\t\n748\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n749\t        if result.is_valid() {\n750\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n751\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n752\t\n753\t            // Merge errors from detector\n754\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n755\t                result.valid = false;\n756\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n757\t            }\n758\t        }\n759\t\n760\t        result\n761\t    }\n762\t\n763\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n764\t        // Read file content\n765\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n766\t            Ok(content) => content,\n767\t            Err(err) => {\n768\t                return ValidationResult {\n769\t                    valid: false,\n770\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n771\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n772\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n773\t                    )],\n774\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n775\t                };\n776\t            }\n777\t        };\n778\t\n779\t        // Validate the content\n780\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n781\t    }\n782\t\n783\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n784\t        &self.config\n785\t    }\n786\t\n787\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n788\t    where\n789\t        Self: Sized,\n790\t    {\n791\t        Self {\n792\t            config,\n793\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n794\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n795\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n796\t        }\n797\t    }\n798\t}\n799\t\n800\t// ============================================================================\n801\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n802\t// ============================================================================\n803\t\n804\t#[cfg(test)]\n805\tmod integration_tests {\n806\t    use super::*;\n807\t\n808\t    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n809\t    #[test]\n810\t    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n811\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n812\t      let value = result.unwrap();\n827\t        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n828\t        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n829\t    }\n830\t\n831\t    /// Test parse_str with deeply 8080\n866\t  database:\n867\t    host: db.example.com\n868\t    port: 5432\n869\t  cache:\n870\t    host: redis.example.com\n871\t    port: 6379\n872\t\"#;\n873\t\n874\t        let result = #[test]\n1063\t    fn test_real_world_config_scenario() {\n1064\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n1065\t\n1066\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1067\tapplication:\n1068\t  name: myapp\n1069\t }\n1104\t\n1105\t    /// Test parse_str with document markers\n1106\t    #[test]\n1107\t    fn test_parse_str_with_document_markers() {\n1108\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n1109\t\n1110\t BasicParser::new();\n1592\t\n1593\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1594\tkey1: value1\n1595\t  nested:\n1596\t    deep: value\n1597\tkey2: value2\n1598\t\"#;\n1599\t\n1600\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1601\t\n1602\t        // Check that all transitions are properly classified\n1603\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1604\t            let transition_type = transition.transition_type();\n1605\t\n1606\t            // Verify classification matches indent change\n1607\t            if transition.to_indent > transition.from_indent {\n1608\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1609\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1610\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1611\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1612\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1613\t            } else {\n1614\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1615\t                           \"No indent change should be classified as SameLevel\");\n1616\t            }\n1617\t        }\n1618\t    }\n1619\t\n1620\t    /// Test scope operation mapping\n1621\t    #[test]\n1622\t    fn test_scope_operation_mapping() {\n1623\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1624\t\n1625\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1626\tparent:\n1627\t  child: value\n1628\tsibling: value2\n1629\t\"#;\n1630\t\n1631\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1632\t\n1633\t        // Verify each transition has a valid scope operation\n1634\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1635\t            let \"exit-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_exit_scope()),\n1640\t                \"stay-in-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_same_level()),\n1641\t                _ => panic!(\"Invalid scope operation: {}\", operation),\n1642\t            }\n1643\t        }\n1644\t    }\n1645\t\n1646\t    /// Test transition counts by type\n1647\t    #[test]\n1648\t    fn test_transition_counts() {\n1649\t    \"Transition {} should be after previous transition\", i);\n1841\t            last_line = transition.line_number;\n1842\t        }\n1843\t\n1844\t        // Each transition should have complete
